title: podman kube generate --service keeps using nodePort in pod spec
body:
### Issue Description

I ran an nginx container:

`podman run -d -p 9000:80 --name ng nginx`

then ask podman to generate Kubernetes manifests with service:

`podman kube generate ng --service`



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `podman run -d -p 9000:80 --name ng nginx`
2. `podman kube generate ng --service`


### Describe the results you received

Got the following output:

```
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-4.3.1
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2023-02-16T16:26:43Z"
  labels:
    app: ng-pod
  name: ng-pod
spec:
  ports:
  - name: "80"
    nodePort: 31078
    port: 80
    targetPort: 80
  selector:
    app: ng-pod
  type: NodePort
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  annotations:
    io.podman.annotations.autoremove/ng: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.init/ng: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.privileged/ng: "FALSE"
    io.podman.annotations.publish-all/ng: "FALSE"
  creationTimestamp: "2023-02-16T16:26:43Z"
  labels:
    app: ng-pod
  name: ng-pod
spec:
  automountServiceAccountToken: false
  containers:
  - args:
    - nginx
    - -g
    - daemon off;
    image: docker.io/library/nginx:latest
    name: ng
    ports:
    - containerPort: 80
      hostPort: 9000
    securityContext:
      capabilities:
        drop:
        - CAP_MKNOD
        - CAP_NET_RAW
        - CAP_AUDIT_WRITE
  enableServiceLinks: false
``

The pod spec still has the `hostPost` which is an issue on constrained environments

### Describe the results you expected

The hostPort should not be part of the pod seoc

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
It has been fixed by https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/17501
Probably your client/podman is not using yet this very recent update
